text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data instead of being explicitly programmed. In supervised learning, the computer is given a set of training data. This data includes examples with the correct answers. The computer uses these examples to build a model, which is like a set of rules or patterns it discovers. Once the model is built, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For instance, it might predict house prices or whether an email is spam. The accuracy of these predictions is measured by comparing them to the correct answers. The better the training data, the more accurate the model will be.
